contents: Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.PathGroupoids Basics.Trunc

Require Import Basics.Overture Basics.Tactics Basics.PathGroupoids Basics.Trunc
  Basics.Iff Basics.Equivalences Basics.Predicate.

Require Import Groups.Group AbGroups.Abelianization AbGroups.AbelianGroup
  Groups.QuotientGroup.

(** Commutators in groups *)

(** ** Commutators of group elements *)

(** Note that this convention is chosen due to the convention we chose for group conjugation elsewhere. *)
Definition grp_commutator {G : Group} (x y : G) : G := x * y * x^ * y^.
Notation "[ x , y ]" := (grp_commutator x y) : mc_mult_scope.
Arguments grp_commutator {G} x y : simpl never.

(** ** Easy properties of commutators *)

(** Commuting elements of a group have trivial commutator. *)

Definition grp_commutator_commutes {G : Group} (x y : G) (p : x * y = y * x)
  : [x, y] = 1.
(** A commutator of an element with itself is the unit. *)

Definition grp_commutator_cancel {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : [x, x] = 1.
(** A commutator of a group element with unit on the left is the unit. *)
(** A commutator of a group element with unit on the right is the unit. *)
(** Commutators in abelian groups are trivial. *)

Definition ab_commutator (A : AbGroup) (x y : A)
  : [x, y] = 1.
(** The commutator can be thought of as the "error" in the commutativity of two elements of a group. *)

Definition grp_commutator_swap_op {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : x * y = [x, y] * y * x.

goals.conclusion: x * y = x * y * x^ * y^ * y * x

contents: (** Inverting a commutator reverses the order. *)

Definition grp_commutator_inv {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : [x, y]^ = [y, x].

Definition grp_homo_commutator {G H : Group} (f : G $-> H) (x y : G)
  : f [x, y] = [f x, f y].

goals.conclusion: f (x * y * x^ * y^) = f x * f y * (f x)^ * (f y)^

contents: (** A commutator with a product on the left side can be expanded as the product of a conjugated commutator and another commutator. *)

Definition grp_commutator_op_l {G : Group} (x y z : G)
  : [x * y, z] = grp_conj x [y, z] * [x, z].

goals.conclusion: [x * y, z] = grp_conj x [y, z] * [x, z]
[x * y, z] = grp_conj x [y, z] * [x, z]

contents: (** A commutator with a product on the right side can be expanded as the product of a conjugated commutator and another commutator. *)

Definition grp_commutator_op_r {G : Group} (x y z : G)
  : [x, y * z] = [x, y] * grp_conj y [x, z].

goals.conclusion: [x, y * z] = [x, y] * grp_conj y [x, z]
[x, y * z] = [x, y] * grp_conj y [x, z]

contents: (** A commutator with the element on the right being multiplied on the right gives a conjugation. *)

Definition grp_op_l_commutator {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : [x, y] * y = grp_conj x y.

goals.conclusion: x * y * x^ * y^ * y = x * y * x^

contents: (** A commutator with the inverse element on the left being multiplied on the left gives a conjugation. *)

Definition grp_op_r_commutator {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : x^ * [x, y] = grp_conj y x^.
(** Commutators with inverted left sides are conjugated commutators. *)

Definition grp_commutator_inv_l {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : [x^, y] = grp_conj x^ [y, x].
(** Commutators with inverted right sides are conjugated commutators. *)

Definition grp_commutator_inv_r {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : [x, y^] = grp_conj y^ [y, x].

Definition grp_conj_commutator_inv_l {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : grp_conj x [x^, y] = [y, x].

Definition grp_conj_commutator_inv_r {G : Group} (x y : G)
  : grp_conj y [x, y^] = [y, x].

(** ** Hall-Witt identity *)

(** The Hall-Witt identity is a Jacobi-like identity for groups. It states that the different commutators of 3 elements (with one conjugated) assemble into an identity. The proof is purely mechanical and simply cancels all the terms. This is a rare instance where a mechanized proof is much shorter than an on-paper proof. *)

Definition grp_hall_witt {G : Group} (x y z : G)
  : [[x, y], grp_conj y z] * [[y, z], grp_conj z x] * [[z, x], grp_conj x y] = 1.

goals.conclusion: [[x, y], grp_conj y z] * [[y, z], grp_conj z x] * [[z, x], grp_conj x y] = 1
[[x, y], grp_conj y z] * [[y, z], grp_conj z x] * [[z, x], grp_conj x y] = 1

contents: (** This variant of the Hall-Witt identity is useful later for proving the three subgroups lemma. *)

Definition grp_hall_witt' {G : Group} (x y z : G)
  : grp_conj x [[x^, y], z] * grp_conj z [[z^, x], y] * grp_conj y [[y^, z], x] = 1.

goals.conclusion:
grp_conj x [[x^, y], z] * grp_conj z [[z^, x], y] * grp_conj y [[y^, z], x] =
1

grp_conj x [[x^, y], z] * grp_conj z [[z^, x], y] * grp_conj y [[y^, z], x] =
1

[grp_conj x [x^, y], grp_conj x z] * [grp_conj z [z^, x], grp_conj z y] *
[grp_conj y [y^, z], grp_conj y x] = 1
[[y, x], grp_conj x z] * [[x, z], grp_conj z y] * [[z, y], grp_conj y x] = 1

contents:
Instance issubgroup_precomp_commutator_l {G : Group} (H : Subgroup G)
  `{!IsNormalSubgroup H} (y : G)
  : IsSubgroup (fun x => H [x, y]).

goals.conclusion: forall x y0 : G, H [x, y] -> H [y0, y] -> H [x * y0, y]
forall x : G, H [x, y] -> H [x^, y]
forall x y0 : G, H [x, y] -> H [y0, y] -> H [x * y0, y]
forall x : G, H [x, y] -> H [x^, y]

contents: (** The condition [H [x, y]] is equivalent to the condition [H [y, x]]. *)

Definition issubgroup_precomp_commutator_agree {G : Group} (H : Subgroup G)
  (x y : G)
  : H [x, y] <~> H [y, x].
(** So we get this symmetrical version as well. *)

Instance issubgroup_precomp_commutator_r {G : Group} (H : Subgroup G)
  `{!IsNormalSubgroup H} (x : G)
  : IsSubgroup (fun y => H [x, y])
  := issubgroup_equiv (fun y => issubgroup_precomp_commutator_agree H y x) _.

Definition subgroup_precomp_commutator_l {G : Group} (H : Subgroup G) (y : G)
  `{!IsNormalSubgroup H}
  : Subgroup G
  := Build_Subgroup _ (fun x => H [x, y]) _.

Definition subgroup_precomp_commutator_r {G : Group} (H : Subgroup G) (x : G)
  `{!IsNormalSubgroup H}
  : Subgroup G
  := Build_Subgroup _ (fun y => H [x, y]) _.

Definition subgroup_commutator {G : Group@{i}} (H K : Subgroup@{i i} G)
  : Subgroup@{i i} G
  := subgroup_generated (fun g => exists (x : H) (y : K), [x.1, y.1] = g).
Notation "[ H , K ]" := (subgroup_commutator H K) : group_scope.
(** [subgroup_commutator H K] is the smallest subgroup containing the commutators of elements of [H] with elements of [K]. *)

Definition subgroup_commutator_rec {G : Group} {H K : Subgroup G} (J : Subgroup G)
  (i : forall x y, H x -> K y -> J (grp_commutator x y))
  : [H, K] ⊆ J.

hypotheses.type: forall x y : G, H x -> K y -> J (grp_commutator x y)
forall x y : G, H x -> K y -> J (grp_commutator x y)

contents:
rapply subgroup_generated_rec; intros x [[y Hy] [[z Kz] p]];
    destruct p; simpl.

hypotheses.type: forall x y0 : G, H x -> K y0 -> J (grp_commutator x y0)

contents: (** Doubly iterated [subgroup_commutator]s of [H], [J] and [K] are the smallest of the normal subgroups containing the doubly iterated commutators of elements of [H], [J] and [K]. *)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z : G,
H x -> J y -> K z -> N (grp_commutator (grp_commutator x y) z)

forall x y z : G,
H x -> J y -> K z -> N (grp_commutator (grp_commutator x y) z)

goals.conclusion: forall x y : G, [H, J] x -> K y -> N (grp_commutator x y)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z : G,
H x -> J y -> K z -> N (grp_commutator (grp_commutator x y) z)

contents: intros x z HJx Kz; revert x HJx.

goals.conclusion: forall x : G, [H, J] x -> N (grp_commutator x z)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z0 : G,
H x -> J y -> K z0 -> N (grp_commutator (grp_commutator x y) z0)

contents: change (N (grp_commutator ?x z)) with (subgroup_precomp_commutator_l N z x).

goals.conclusion: forall x : G, [H, J] x -> subgroup_precomp_commutator_l N z x

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z0 : G,
H x -> J y -> K z0 -> N (grp_commutator (grp_commutator x y) z0)

goals.conclusion:
forall x y : G,
H x -> J y -> subgroup_precomp_commutator_l N z (grp_commutator x y)

hypotheses.type:
forall x y z0 : G,
H x -> J y -> K z0 -> N (grp_commutator (grp_commutator x y) z0)

contents: (** A commutator of elements from each respective subgroup is in the commutator subgroup. *)

goals.conclusion: {x0 : H & {y0 : J & grp_commutator x0.1 y0.1 = grp_commutator x y}}
forall x y : G, H x -> J y -> [K, L] (grp_commutator x y)

contents:
Definition subgroup_incl_commutator_symm {G : Group} (H J : Subgroup G)
  : [H, J] ⊆ [J, H].

goals.conclusion: forall x y : G, H x -> J y -> [J, H] (grp_commutator x y)

contents: (** Commutator subgroups are symmetric in their arguments. *)

Definition subgroup_commutator_symm {G : Group} (H J : Subgroup G)
  : [H, J] ↔ [J, H].
(** The opposite subgroup of a commutator subgroup is the commutator subgroup of the opposite subgroups. *)

Definition subgroup_eq_commutator_grp_op {G : Group} (H J : Subgroup G)
  : subgroup_grp_op [H, J] ↔ [subgroup_grp_op J, subgroup_grp_op H].

goals.conclusion:
(fun g : G => {x : H & {y : J & grp_commutator x.1 y.1 = g}})
↔ (fun g : grp_op G =>
   {x : subgroup_grp_op J &
   {y : subgroup_grp_op H & grp_commutator x.1 y.1 = g}})

(fun y : J => grp_commutator a.1 y.1 = x) a0 <~>
(fun b : subgroup_grp_op J =>
 grp_commutator b.1
   (grp_iso_subgroup_group (grp_op_iso_inv G)
      (fun x0 : G => equiv_subgroup_inv (subgroup_grp_op H) x0) a).1 =
 x)
  (grp_iso_subgroup_group (grp_op_iso_inv G)
     (fun x0 : G => equiv_subgroup_inv (subgroup_grp_op J) x0) a0)
grp_commutator a.1 a0.1 = x <~> grp_commutator (a0.1)^ (a.1)^ = x

contents: (** A commutator subgroup of an abelian group is always trivial. *)

Definition istrivial_commutator_subgroup_ab {A : AbGroup} (H K : Subgroup A)
  : IsTrivialGroup [H, K].
(** A commutator of normal subgroups is normal. *)

goals.conclusion: forall x y : G, [H, J] x -> [H, J] (y * x * y^)
forall x : G, [H, J] x -> [H, J] (y * x * y^)

(fun g : G => {x : H & {y0 : J & grp_commutator x.1 y0.1 = g}})
⊆ (fun x : G => {x0 : H & {y0 : J & grp_commutator x0.1 y0.1 = grp_conj y x}})

{x0 : H & {y0 : J & grp_commutator x0.1 y0.1 = x}} ->
{x0 : H & {y0 : J & grp_commutator x0.1 y0.1 = grp_conj y x}}

grp_commutator a.1 a0.1 = x ->
grp_commutator (grp_iso_normal_conj H y a).1 (grp_iso_normal_conj J y a0).1 =
grp_conj y x

grp_commutator (grp_iso_normal_conj H y a).1 (grp_iso_normal_conj J y a0).1 =
grp_conj y x

grp_conj y (grp_commutator (subgroup_incl H a) (subgroup_incl J a0)) =
grp_conj y x

contents: (** Commutator subgroups distribute over products of normal subgroups on the left. *)

goals.conclusion: [subgroup_product H K, L] ↔ subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L]
[subgroup_product H K, L] ↔ subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L]
[subgroup_product H K, L] x -> subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L] x
subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L] x -> [subgroup_product H K, L] x
[subgroup_product H K, L] x -> subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L] x

forall x y : G,
subgroup_product H K x ->
L y -> subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L] (grp_commutator x y)

contents: intros x y HKx Ly; revert x HKx.

goals.conclusion:
forall x : G,
subgroup_product H K x -> subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L] (grp_commutator x y)

contents:
change (subgroup_product ?H ?J (grp_commutator ?x y))
      with (subgroup_precomp_commutator_l (subgroup_product H J) y x).

goals.conclusion:
forall x : G,
subgroup_product H K x ->
subgroup_precomp_commutator_l (subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L]) y x

pred_or H K
⊆ subgroup_precomp_commutator_l (subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L]) y
subgroup_product [H, L] [K, L] x -> [subgroup_product H K, L] x
pred_or [H, L] [K, L] ⊆ [subgroup_product H K, L]
forall x : G, [H, L] x -> [subgroup_product H K, L] x
forall x : G, [K, L] x -> [subgroup_product H K, L] x

contents: (** Commutator subgroups distribute over products of normal subgroups on the right. *)

goals.conclusion: [H, subgroup_product K L] ↔ subgroup_product [H, K] [H, L]
[H, subgroup_product K L] ↔ subgroup_product [H, K] [H, L]
[H, subgroup_product K L] x <-> subgroup_product [H, K] [H, L] x
[subgroup_product K L, H] x <-> subgroup_product [H, K] [H, L] x
[subgroup_product K L, H] x <-> subgroup_product [K, H] [L, H] x

contents: (** The subgroup image of a commutator is included in the commutator of the subgroup images. The converse only generally holds for a normal [J] and [K] and a surjective [f]. *)

goals.conclusion: subgroup_image f [J, K] ⊆ [subgroup_image f J, subgroup_image f K]
subgroup_image f [J, K] ⊆ [subgroup_image f J, subgroup_image f K]
[J, K] ⊆ subgroup_preimage f [subgroup_image f J, subgroup_image f K]

forall x y : G,
J x ->
K y ->
subgroup_preimage f [subgroup_image f J, subgroup_image f K]
  (grp_commutator x y)

subgroup_preimage f [subgroup_image f J, subgroup_image f K]
  (grp_commutator x y)
[subgroup_image f J, subgroup_image f K] (f (grp_commutator x y))
[subgroup_image f J, subgroup_image f K] (grp_commutator (f x) (f y))

contents:
(** ** Derived subgroup *)

(** The commutator subgroup of the maximal subgroup with itself is called the derived subgroup. It is the subgroup generated by all commutators. *)

Definition normalsubgroup_derived G : NormalSubgroup G
  := Build_NormalSubgroup G [G, G] _.
(** We can quotient a group by its derived subgroup. *)

Definition grp_derived_quotient (G : Group) : Group
  := QuotientGroup G (normalsubgroup_derived G).
(** We can show that the multiplication then becomes commutative. *)

goals.conclusion: forall y : grp_derived_quotient G, x * y = y * x

contents: srapply grp_quotient_ind_hprop; intros y; revert x.

goals.conclusion:
forall x : grp_derived_quotient G,
(fun x0 : G / normalsubgroup_derived G => x * x0 = x0 * x)
  (grp_quotient_map y)

contents: srapply grp_quotient_ind_hprop; intros x; cbn beta.

goals.conclusion:
grp_quotient_map x * grp_quotient_map y =
grp_quotient_map y * grp_quotient_map x

contents: apply qglue, tr, sgt_in; exists (y^; tt), (x^; tt).
(** Hence we have a way of producing abelian groups from groups. *)

Definition abgroup_derived_quotient (G : Group) : AbGroup
  := Build_AbGroup (grp_derived_quotient G) _.
(** We get a recursion principle into abelian groups. *)

Definition abgroup_derived_quotient_rec {G : Group} {A : AbGroup} (f : G $-> A)
  : abgroup_derived_quotient G $-> A.

goals.conclusion: forall n : G, normalsubgroup_derived G n -> f n = 1

forall n : G,
normalsubgroup_derived G n -> subgroup_preimage f (trivial_subgroup A) n

forall x y : G,
maximal_subgroup G x ->
maximal_subgroup G y ->
subgroup_preimage f (trivial_subgroup A) (grp_commutator x y)

contents: (** Furthermore, this construction is an abelianization. *)

Definition isabelianization_derived_quotient (G : Group)
  : IsAbelianization (abgroup_derived_quotient G) grp_quotient_map.

goals.conclusion:
forall x y : abgroup_derived_quotient G $-> A,
group_precomp A grp_quotient_map x $== group_precomp A grp_quotient_map y ->
x $== y

{a : abgroup_derived_quotient G $-> A &
group_precomp A grp_quotient_map a $== f}

forall x y : abgroup_derived_quotient G $-> A,
group_precomp A grp_quotient_map x $== group_precomp A grp_quotient_map y ->
x $== y

hypotheses.type: group_precomp A grp_quotient_map f $== group_precomp A grp_quotient_map g

contents: srapply grp_quotient_ind_hprop; intros x; cbn beta.

hypotheses.type: group_precomp A grp_quotient_map f $== group_precomp A grp_quotient_map g

Definition three_subgroups_lemma (G : Group) (H J K N : Subgroup G)
  `{!IsNormalSubgroup N}
  (T1 : [[H, J], K] ⊆ N) (T2 : [[J, K], H] ⊆ N)
  : [[K, H], J] ⊆ N.

goals.conclusion:
forall x y z : G,
K x -> H y -> J z -> N (grp_commutator (grp_commutator x y) z)
forall x y z : G, K x -> H y -> J z -> N [[x, y], z]

contents: intros z x y Kz Hx Jy.
pose proof (grp_hall_witt' x y z^) as p.

hypotheses.type:
grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^] * grp_conj z^ [[(z^)^, x], y] *
grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x] = 1

grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^] * grp_conj z^ [[z, x], y] * grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x] =
1

grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^] * grp_conj z^ [[z, x], y] =
(grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x])^

grp_conj z^ [[z, x], y] =
(grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^])^ * (grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x])^

(grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^])^ * (grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x])^

goals.conclusion: {x0 : subgroup_commutator J K & {y0 : H & [x0.1, y0.1] = [[y^, z^], x]}}

hypotheses.type:
grp_conj z^ [[z, x], y] =
(grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^])^ * (grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x])^

grp_conj z^ [[z, x], y] =
(grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^])^ * (grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x])^

grp_conj z^ [[z, x], y] =
(grp_conj x [[x^, y], z^])^ * (grp_conj y [[y^, z^], x])^

contents:
Definition three_trivial_commutators (G : Group) (H J K : Subgroup G)
  : IsTrivialGroup [[H, J], K]
    -> IsTrivialGroup [[J, K], H]
    -> IsTrivialGroup [[K, H], J].

goals.conclusion:
IsTrivialGroup [[H, J], K] ->
IsTrivialGroup [[J, K], H] -> IsTrivialGroup [[K, H], J]

IsTrivialGroup [[H, J], K] ->
IsTrivialGroup [[J, K], H] -> IsTrivialGroup [[K, H], J]
